data.title: Performance Food Group works out a board deal with activist investor Sachem Head
data.description:
Performance Food Group Company (NYSE:PFGC) disclosed that it entered a cooperation agreement with activist investor Sachem Head Capital Management LP.

The terms of the cooperation agreement include the board increasing to thirteen directors, with the appointment of Sachem Head Scott Ferguson. He will be added to the board's Audit and Finance Committee, which it noted was where all proposed M&A transactions and other strategic alternatives are discussed.  

For its part, Sachem Head has agreed to a customary standstill, voting, and confidentiality commitments, among other provisions. 

"This outcome is the result of constructive engagement with Scott and his team and demonstrates our board’s openness to fresh perspectives," Performance Food CEO George Holm said in the statement.

Less than a month ago, Ferguson's Sachem Head Capital Management nominated four director candidates to Performance Food's board (NYSE:PFGC). Just last week, Performance Food (PFGC) and US Foods (USFD) signed an agreement to exchange sensitive financial information. 

Members of the Performance Food Group Company (PFGC) board and management team had engaged with several of the company's large stockholders to hear their perspectives. The company also engaged with US Foods (USFD) on how the two companies could most effectively explore a potential business combination while safeguarding confidential information.

Following the conversations, the Performance Food Group Company (PFGC) board concluded that there was sufficient basis to begin information sharing. The companies are therefore initiating a clean team process consisting of a group of independent lawyers, economists, and consultants who will perform analysis using more detailed confidential information.

Shares of Performance Food Group Company (PFGC) were up 0.4% in premarket trading.

data.title: Jiuzi’s board endorses crypto asset investment policy allowing up to $1B deployment
data.description:
• Jiuzi Holdings (NASDAQ:JZXN) said on Wednesday that its board has formally approved and adopted a Crypto Asset Investment Policy. 
  • This policy authorizes the company to allocate portions of its cash reserves into select crypto assets within a risk management framework.
  • This strategic initiative follows the recent appointment of industry-renowned crypto expert Doug Buerger as Chief Operating Officer (COO). 
  • The board has authorized the company to deploy up to US$1B in funds for purchasing crypto assets, ensuring controlled exposure to risk.
  • In its initial phase, investments will be limited to Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), and BNB. The company will not self-custody acquired crypto assets.
  • A "Crypto Asset Risk Committee" has been established under the leadership of CFO Huijie Gao. This committee will oversee policy implementation and report regularly to the board.

data.title: Petrobras, Exxon ask Brazil's antitrust regulator to intervene in Subsea7-Saipem merger
data.description:
Petrobras (NYSE:PBR), Exxon Mobil (XOM) and TechnipFMC (FTI) have petitioned Brazil's antitrust regulator Cade to oppose a merger between Italy’s Saipem (OTCPK:SAPMF) (OTCPK:SAPMY) and Norway’s Subsea7 (OTCPK:SUBCY), Reuters reported late Tuesday.

In its filing with Cade, Petrobras (NYSE:PBR) said the merger would impact the company, since nearly half of the total number of vessels available for servicing its subsea engineering, procurement, construction, and installation contracts belong to Saipem (OTCPK:SAPMF) (OTCPK:SAPMY) and Subsea7 (OTCPK:SUBCY).

Exxon (XOM) said a merger would lead to a high concentration of contractors offering subsea umbilicals, risers and flowlines - or SURF projects - reducing competition and customer choice, and TechnipFMC (FTI) said the deal would virtually eliminate opportunities for competitors in Brazilian public tenders.

The companies want Cade to block the merger or impose remedies to preserve competition in Brazil, such as asset sales, according to the report.

The merger deal announced earlier this year would create Saipem7, a company with ~€21 billion in revenue and a combined backlog of €43 billion.

data.title: uniQure announces refinancing of $50M debt and secures additional $125M in non-dilutive funding
data.description:
• uniQure (NASDAQ:QURE) said on Wednesday that it entered into a $175M non-dilutive senior secured term loan facility with Hercules Capital (HTGC). 
  • The transaction is designed to enhance the company’s financial flexibility to fund the potential commercial launch of AMT-130, its investigational gene therapy for the treatment of Huntington’s disease.
  • The loan facility consists of three tranches, including a first tranche of $50M refinancing the current debt outstanding at closing and an additional term loan tranche of $100M, which can be drawn at the company’s option subject to the achievement of both a regulatory milestone related to AMT-130 and a financial milestone. 
  • A third tranche of up to $25M is available, subject to Hercules’ approval. 
  • All tranches have a floating interest rate of the greater of 9.45% and the prime rate plus 2.45%, reflecting a current floating rate of 9.70%, compared to the current floating interest rate prior to the refinancing, which would have been 11.95%. The tranches mature in October 2030.

data.title: First Mining expands newly discovered Miroir target
data.description:
First Mining Gold (FF:CA) (OTCQX:FFMGF) on Wednesday announced additional drilling results from the 2025 drilling program at its Duparquet Gold Project located in the Abitibi region of Quebec, Canada.

The company is actively drilling at the Duparquet Project through the fall and has completed ~16,000 m of drilling to date, with a continued focus on advancing potential resource growth targets. 

Drilling at the Miroir target, located 225 m north of the Central Duparquet - Valentre - Dumico target, continues to build strong continuity, returning multiple robust gold intercepts near surface.

data.title: PayPal to sell ~$7B of buy now, pay later receivables to Blue Owl Capital funds
data.description:
PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) and Blue Owl Capital (NYSE:OWL) said on Wednesday that funds managed by the alternative asset manager will acquire ~$7B worth of PayPal's buy now, pay later receivables.

PYPL shares were +1.25% pre-market to $68.15, while OWL was +0.48% to $18.73.

The two companies have executed a two-year agreement.

PayPal will remain responsible for all customer-facing activities, including underwriting and servicing, associated with its U.S. 'Pay in 4' buy now, pay later products.

Pay in 4 allows consumers to pay for eligible purchases in four interest-free payments over six weeks.

The payments giant said the transaction is already contemplated in its third quarter and full year 2025 guidance.

data.title: Poland's zloty slumps as Trump backs use of force against Russian jets
data.description:
Poland’s zloty slipped to its weakest level this month after U.S. President Donald Trump said NATO members should shoot down Russian aircraft violating allied airspace, raising fears of escalation.

The currency fell 0.2% against the euro on Wednesday, underperforming peers such as the Hungarian forint and Czech koruna. Trump’s remarks, quickly echoed by Polish Foreign Minister Radoslaw Sikorski, followed repeated Russian drone and jet incursions into NATO territory.

Analysts said investors worry a Russian plane could be downed if tensions flare. Prime Minister Donald Tusk has already vowed to defend Polish skies after nearly 20 drones crossed into its airspace in one night.

Despite recent volatility, the zloty remains steady versus the euro this year and is up 14% against the dollar.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Therma Bright and Gen-X Med partner to distribute Venowave™
